Description: Out For Season (Foot) - The Nets reported Egor Demin will miss rest of season with plantar fasciitis.
Out For Season (Hamstring) - Porter (hamstring) has been ruled out for the remainder of the 2025-26 season, according to Erik Slater of ClutchPoints.com.
Out For Season (Thumb) - The Nets said on Thursday (Mar. 12) that Sharpe will undergo season-ending thumb surgery.
Out For Season (Ankle) - The Nets announced Friday that Wolf (ankle) is being shut down for the rest of the season, according to Erik Slater of ClutchPoints.com.
Out For Season (Ankle) - The Hornets announced on Thursday (Apr. 9) that Hall underwent a procedure to address a fractured right ankle and will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 campaign.
Out For Season (Toe) - The Bulls said on Saturday (Feb. 21) that Collins will undergo surgery for a sprain right toe and will be sidelined for the rest of the 2025-26 season.
Out For Season (Shoulder) - The Bulls reported on Wednesday (Dec. 3) that Essengue will have shoulder surgery and miss the remainder of the season, per HC Billy Donovan.
Out For Season (Calf) - Smith will miss the remainder of the season, according to Joel Lorenzi of The Athletic.
Out For Season (Knee) - The Mavericks said on Wednesday (Feb. 18) that Irving is OUT for the season, per ESPN's Shams Charania.
Out For Season (Foot) - The Mavericks announced on Sunday (Dec. 21) that Lively II has successfully underwent foot surgery.
Out For Season (Knee) - Butler's injury from Monday (Jan. 19) Night's game involves a torn right ACL and he will be out for the rest of the season, as per Shams Charania of ESPN.
Out For Season (Knee) - The Warriors reported guard Moses Moody has been diagnosed with a torn patellar tendon in his left knee, and is out for the season.
Out For Season (Ankle) - The Rockets said on Wednesday (Jan. 28) that Adams underwent season-ending ankle surgery.
Out For Season (ACL) - The Rockets said on Monday (Sept. 22) that VanVleet suffered a torn ACL and may be out for the entire 2025-26 campaign, per Shams Charania of ESPN.
Out For Season (Knee) - Furphy was diagnosed with a torn right ACL on Monday and will miss the remainder of the season, Michael Scotto of USA Today

Out For Season (Achilles) - The Pacers said on Tuesday (Jan. 27) that Haliburton will likely be in line for a relatively normal offseason and should be free of restrictions for the start of training camp next fall.
Out For Season (Rib) - The Pacers said on Friday (Mar. 20) that Zubac will miss the rest of the season with a fractured rib.
Out For Season (Hip) - Beal will undergo season-ending surgery on his left hip, as per ESPN's Shams Charania reports.

Out For Season (Foot) - Niederhauser has been diagnosed with a Lisfranc injury in his right foot and will require surgery. He's been ruled out for the remainder of the 2025-26 season, according to Joey Linn of SI.com.

Out For Season (Knee) - The Grizzlies said on Sunday (Mar. 15) that Aldama will undergo a season-ending arthroscopic procedure and receive an orthobiologic injection to address discomfort in his right knee, according to Brett Siegel of ClutchPoints.com.

Out For Season (Finger) - The Grizzlies announced Thursday that Caldwell-Pope underwent surgery to address the misalignment of his right pinky finger and will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 campaign.
Out For Season (Ankle) - The Grizzlies said that Edey will miss the remainder of the season.
Out For Season (Elbow) - The Grizzlies announced on Tuesday (Mar. 24) that Morant will miss the remainder of the season.

Out For Season (Toe) - The Grizzlies said on Friday (Mar. 13) that Pippen Jr. will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 season after undergoing a sesamoidectomy to address ongoing discomfort in his right big toe, per Michael Scotto of USA Today.
Out For Season (Toe) - The Grizzlies announced Tuesday that Wells underwent a successful procedure to stabilize his right great toe and will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 campaign.
Out For Season (Knee) - Bucks head coach Doc Rivers said on Saturday (Mar. 28) that he would be surprised if Porter played again this season, per Eric Nehm of The Athletic.
Out For Season (Leg) - DiVincenzo has been diagnosed with a torn right Achilles tendon, and he will miss the remainder of the postseason, per Shams Charania of ESPN reports.
Out For Season (Knee) - The Thunder announced on Friday (Sept. 5) that Sorber will miss the 2025-2026 season with a torn ACL.
Out For Season (Achilles) - The Trail Blazers said on Thursday (Sept. 25) that Lillard won't be back on the court for the 2025-26 season due to his achilles injury.
Out For Season (Thumb) - Eubanks will have surgery to repair a torn UCL in his left thumb, according to Matt George of ABC 10 Sacramento reports Sunday (Mar. 15).
Out For Season (Eye) - Hunter is set to undergo season-ending eye surgery, according to Chris Haynes of NBA TV.
Out For Season (Finger) - LaVine will undergo season-ending surgery on his right hand after the All-Star break, per Chris Haynes of NBA TV.
Out For Season (Back) - Sabonis underwent season-ending knee surgery Wednesday (Feb. 18) to repair a torn left meniscus, Shams Charania of ESPN.

Out For Season (Ankle) - The Spurs announced on Wednesday (Feb. 4) that Jones Garcia underwent ankle surgery and will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 season, per Tom Orsborn of the San Antonio Express-News.
Out For Season (Knee) - The Jazz said on Thursday (Feb. 12) that Jackson Jr. will miss the rest of the season to undergo knee surgery.
Out For Season (Shoulder) - Kessler will undergo left shoulder surgery to address a torn labrum and will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 season, as per Tony Jones of The Athletic.
Out For Season (Nose) - The Jazz reported on Monday (Feb. 23) that Nurkic will undergo season-ending nose surgery.
Out For Season (Finger) - Head coach Brian Keefe said Tuesday that Davis is not expected to play again this season, according to Chase Hughes of Monumental Sports Network.
Out For Season (Elbow) - The Wizards announced on Thursday (Apr. 2) that George will miss the rest of the season.

Out For Season (Shoulder) - The Wizards announced Wednesday (Jan. 15) that Whitmore will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 season while he begins the recovery process for a diagnosed venous condition.
Out For Season (Quad) - Young isn't expected to play again in 2025-26, according to Chase Hughes of Monumental Sports Network.
